---
species: "Testudinalia testudinalis"
accepted_code: "TETE"
alternate_codes: []
diminutive: ""
alternate_diminutives: ["", ""]
common_name: "Tortoise limpet"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Coarse pinnate hydroid often found on boulders or epiphytically"
image: '/header_images/testudinalia_testudinalis.jpg'
categories:
  - gastropods
sampling_programs:
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 234208 
---

![](/header_images/testudinalia_testudinalis.jpg)

Photo from O. F. Müller via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=171497&tid=234208)

**Other images:** '/images/TETE_O.F. Müller_worms.jpg'
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Plate limpet that is the shape of a flat cone and can have checkered, radiating lines. Often has a tortoise shell like appearance.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** 

<br/>

**Extended Description:** Sea snail that is found attached to hard substrate in both the intertidal and subtidal. 

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=234208)**
